envVarDocs.title: Telegram Bot Token
envVarDocs.description: Required. Token from BotFather used to connect the bot to Telegram.
envVarDocs.usage: Read at boot in src/lib/config.js and required by src/index.js to start polling.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Create a bot with BotFather and copy the token.

envVarDocs.title: MongoDB Connection String
envVarDocs.description: Required. Stores chat settings, FAQs, suggestions, warns, audit logs, pending inputs, and user default community context.
envVarDocs.usage: Connected once via src/lib/db.js and reused across the bot.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Use MongoDB Atlas or your managed Mongo provider; copy the connection string with a database name.

envVarDocs.title: Global Admin Override IDs
envVarDocs.description: Optional. Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to run admin actions even if they are not a chat admin.
envVarDocs.usage: Parsed in src/lib/config.js and checked in src/services/authz.js.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Get your numeric Telegram user ID (for example via a helper bot).

envVarDocs.title: Log Level
envVarDocs.usage: Read in src/lib/config.js. Defaults to info.

commandDocs.description: Shows a welcome message. In private chat it includes buttons to Rules, FAQ, Suggestion, and Help. In groups it tells users to DM the bot for member tools.
examples: User: /start (DM) → Bot: Welcome message with buttons
User: /start (group) → Bot: Please DM me for member tools
commandDocs.notes: Group onboarding welcomes are automatic when enabled by admins with /welcome_on.

commandDocs.description: Shows categorized member and admin commands and notes about admin permissions.
examples: User: /help → Bot: Members and Admins command list
commandDocs.notes: Admin commands require being a Telegram admin in that chat, unless your ID is in BOT_ADMIN_IDS.

commandDocs.description: Shows the rules for the relevant community. In DMs it uses your default community context (set by running /faq in a group once).
examples: User: /rules (group) → Bot: Rules text
User: /rules (DM) → Bot: Rules for your default community
commandDocs.notes: Admins set rules with /setrules.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Sets or updates rules for the current group. Accepts inline text or prompts for the next message within 60 seconds.
examples: Admin: /setrules Be kind and stay on topic. → Bot: Rules updated.
Admin: /setrules → Bot: Send the rules text as your next message within 60 seconds.
commandDocs.notes: Run this inside the group you want to configure.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Sets the welcome message template for the current group. Supports placeholders {first_name}, {username}, {chat_title}.
examples: Admin: /setwelcome Welcome {first_name} to {chat_title}! → Bot: Welcome template updated.
Admin: /setwelcome → Bot: Send the welcome template as your next message within 60 seconds.
commandDocs.notes: Enable welcomes with /welcome_on.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Enables welcome messages for new members in the current group.
examples: Admin: /welcome_on → Bot: Welcome messages enabled.
commandDocs.notes: Welcome messages are sent on new_chat_members.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Disables welcome messages for new members in the current group.
examples: Admin: /welcome_off → Bot: Welcome messages disabled.

commandDocs.description: In a group: sets your default community context. In DMs: browse FAQ or search when you pass terms.
examples: User: /faq (group) → Bot: Confirms your default community is set for DMs
User: /faq (DM) → Bot: FAQ list
User: /faq wallet setup (DM) → Bot: Top matches
commandDocs.notes: Admins manage FAQ with /faq_add, /faq_edit, /faq_del.

commandDocs.description: In DMs, prompts you to send a suggestion as your next message within 60 seconds.
examples: User: /suggest → Bot: Send your suggestion as your next message within 60 seconds.
commandDocs.notes: Suggestions are stored with status new.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Lists recent suggestions for the current chat (in group) or for your default community (in DM).
examples: Admin: /suggestions → Bot: List of suggestion IDs and statuses

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. Updates a suggestion status (new, accepted, rejected, planned, done).
examples: Admin: /suggest_set a1b2c3 accepted → Bot: Suggestion updated.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only (group). Used as a reply to warn a user. Increments warn count and mentions the threshold.
examples: Admin replies to a message: /warn → Bot: Warned. Count is now 1.
commandDocs.notes: Threshold defaults to 3 and is stored in chat settings.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only (group). Used as a reply to show warn count for a user.
examples: Admin replies: /warns → Bot: Warn count: 2

commandDocs.description: Admin-only (group). Used as a reply to reset warn count for a user.
examples: Admin replies: /resetwarns → Bot: Warns reset.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only (group). Shows last 10 admin actions for the chat.
examples: Admin: /audit → Bot: Last 10 actions
commandDocs.notes: Actions include rules/welcome/warn changes and more.

commandDocs.description: Admin-only. In group: collects announcement text and confirms send to that group. In private: global admins can choose a configured target chat and send a confirmed announcement.
examples: Admin: /announce (group) → Bot: Send announcement text
Global admin: /announce (DM) → Bot: Choose target chat
commandDocs.notes: Sending logs per-target success/failure in server logs.

commandDocs.description: Manage announcement target chats for a global admin account. Lists targets, or add/remove by chatId.
examples: Admin: /announce_targets → Bot: Lists targets
Admin: /announce_targets add -1001234567890 → Bot: Target added.
commandDocs.notes: For safety, non-global admins cannot manage targets in private chat by default.
